elementsOfPerformance.description: Types unify and supersede both existing types.ts files without losing any fields
Zod schemas provide runtime validation for all core entities
RDF vocabulary constants follow semantic web conventions (namespace IRIs, prefixed names)
Relations are first-class entities with direction, ceremony, OCAP, accountability metadata
Package compiles cleanly and exports all modules via barrel index
Constants match exactly what existing UI and server expect

name: chart_1771441903055_desired_outcome
observations: A published @medicine-wheel/ontology-core TypeScript package that serves as the single source of truth for the Medicine Wheel data model — unifying types, constants, Zod schemas, RDF vocabulary, and semantic query helpers across mcp-medicine-wheel (server) and mcp-medicine-wheel-ui (client), grounded in Indigenous relational ontology with first-class relationships, OCAP® governance flags, and Wilson alignment metrics.

name: chart_1771441903055_current_reality
observations: The Medicine Wheel data model is scattered: types.ts in mcp-medicine-wheel defines Direction, RelationalObligation, CeremonyGuidance, DirectionResponse, Tool, Resource, Prompt. lib/types.ts in mcp-medicine-wheel-ui defines RelationalNode, RelationalEdge, CeremonyLog, NarrativeBeat, MedicineWheelCycle plus DIRECTIONS, DIRECTION_COLORS, NODE_TYPE_COLORS, CEREMONY_ICONS constants. These are duplicated and divergent. No central ontology package exists. No Zod validation. No RDF vocabulary. No semantic query helpers. The v0 agent and article both identify ontology-core as the critical next package to build. The mcp-medicine-wheel server has ~50 tools but no formal schema validation layer. Relationships are modeled as simple edges, not first-class entities.
You can revise the ways you name the state / end-result, this is not a todo list
Graph page added to UI navigation bar and Next.js build verified (19kB page)
medicine-wheel-narrative-engine package built: 5 modules (sequencer, cadence, arc, timeline, cycle), 22 exports, clean build, smoke tested
MCP server Redis integrations updated: DirectionName, NodeType, CeremonyType imported from ontology-core replacing inline string unions in relational-memory.ts and narrative-bridge.ts
3 packages now built: ontology-core (published), graph-viz (ready to publish), narrative-engine (ready to publish)
All 25 todos complete across all phases
medicine-wheel-relational-query package built: 3 modules (query, traversal, audit), 13 exports, clean build, smoke tested — graph traversal, OCAP-aware path finding, accountability auditing
medicine-wheel-ui-components package built: 5 React components (DirectionCard, BeatTimeline, NodeInspector, OcapBadge, WilsonMeter), clean build
ALL 5 PACKAGES from v0 roadmap now complete: ontology-core (published), graph-viz (ready), narrative-engine (ready), relational-query (ready), ui-components (ready)
MCP server Redis integrations now import DirectionName, NodeType, CeremonyType from ontology-core
rdfglance Medicine Wheel layout module (medicine_wheel.rs) fully wired into mod.rs with LayoutAlgorithm::MedicineWheel variant — appears in UI Layout dropdown automatically via EnumIter
MW styles module (mw_styles.rs) created with direction colors, backgrounds, strokes, labels, glyphs — aligned with ontology-core DIRECTION_COLORS
MW config module (mw_config.rs) created with configurable direction patterns, display toggles, and ontology-core preset
Integration code in run_layout_algorithm extracts node IRIs and type IRIs from RdfData for direction assignment — same pattern as multipartite layout
Cannot cargo build/check due to no network access for crate downloads — code verified structurally via type/API analysis
All 34 SQL todos complete: 5 roadmap packages + ceremony-protocol + UI enhancements + MCP server integration + rdfglance adaptation
